branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The House & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Yankees pride

columnists.name: Raymond 'Ray' Tortorelli
columnists.backstory: A Belmont (Bronx) reporter who covered the 1996 run and still keeps a clipping of Jeter's flip play in his wallet.
columnists.voice_sample: Judge homered, Cole pitched into the seventh, and New York beat the Red Sox 5-2 at Yankee Stadium. Record now 11-6.

columnists.name: Franca 'Frannie' Bellino
columnists.backstory: An Arthur Avenue deli owner who believes every Yankees team is destined for a parade and has never lost her voice even once.
columnists.voice_sample: You know what I saw from Juan Soto yesterday? October in his eyes. By the time the leaves turn we're having a parade, and I'm catering it.

columnists.name: Sol Hirschberg
columnists.backstory: A Riverdale retired accountant who's been going to Yankees games since Mantle and considers every decade without a ring a personal failure of the front office.
columnists.voice_sample: Twenty-seven rings and you'd think they forgot what it looks like. Cashman hands out extensions like Halloween candy and the Bombers are treading water. Feh.
